message: W-We somehow managed to get it into shape.
But who on earth did such a thing?
The key wasn’t broken, but we need to think of something. Maybe we should put ten locks on it.
I mean, aren’t you worried? This time, nothing was stolen, but...
No, I don’t have anything like that. Cufflinks belong to a man, don’t they?
So, it’s likely the criminal's leftover.
Oh no! Is it already this late!?
Boss, I’m heading to the Guggenheim Museum now.
Do you know where Meryl is?
I'm sorry, but I need to get in touch urgently. Can you do something about this?
I wonder if he’s still there.
Please, let her know that I'm at the location shooting site. So, I’m counting on you.
How one-sided. I feel like a lackey.
I guess there's no choice, although I think she's already gone. I'll check the Art Museum just in case.
It seems I've somehow returned to normal.
I believed that this place would never be broken into, but I guess that was naïve.
It seems nothing was stolen, though. I suppose there are some stupid thieves that target such a poor office.
In this room, the only decoration that could be called one.
It gets suddenly lonely without you.
, I wonder if they are doing things properly without messing up. If Meryl and Cindy find out they are mother and daughter,
It might have been a bit much for them.
I have no idea what kind of things they might say to that pair.
There's no point in thinking about it now.
I can only pray that they handle it well.
If we find out who killed Eva, it might solve the Serial Murder Case as well.
The method feels too sophisticated for a random killer. I wonder if they targeted Eva deliberately from the start?
The incident might have occurred due to some personal grudge. Eva was a woman skilled at being hated by others.
By now, they might be meeting as mother and daughter. I hope they aren't fighting.
I wonder what Meryl will do when she finds out Cindy is her daughter. Will they continue as actresses together?
Will I finally get to see a cheerful Meryl after so long? I really hope this is the last request I get from her.
It was good that we went to the restaurant, but when the waitress merely dropped a knife,
that's right, Kristy suddenly reacted when the knife dropped.
At that time, the knife had fallen from the blade to the floor.
To show an excessive reaction just from seeing that...
Is Kristy afraid of knives or something?
I might need to consult Günter.
I wonder how Cindy will interact with Meryl.
It must be a complex emotional state, after all.
I hope Cindy can forgive Meryl, and they can live happily together as mother and daughter.
As for me, I want the two of them to be happy.
But then, what happens to Tim?
Maybe I should check the scalpel while he’s not around?
If she finds out, her anger is likely to be tremendous. It's better to do it while she's there.
Speaking of which, I still haven't met Tonny. I don't think he’s the culprit, but it's interesting that he had a scalpel.
It seems like Tonny is avoiding me. He probably has a ton of shady stuff going on, but I can't imagine he committed murder.
I wonder how Jennifer is doing?
I'm kind of at a loss, so I think I might go see her.
But even if I meet her, I'm not sure what to do.
I'm actually not grasping what her intentions are, you know.
